folio.label: 140v
folio.l: But knowen for historial thyngö , notable
The sentence of it , sotú is out of doute
This fals Iuge , gotú now fast a boute
To hasten his delyt , al that he may
And so byfel , sone after on a day
This fals Iuge , as telletú vs the storie
As he was wont , sat in his Consistorie
And ©af his domes , vp on sondry cas
This fals Cherl cam fortú , a ful gret pas
And seide lord , if that it be ©oure wille
As dotú me rigút , vp on this pitous bille
In whicú I pleyne , vp on Virginius
And if that he wol seyn , it is nat thus
I wol it preeue , and fynde good wytnesse
That sootú is , that my bille , wol expresse
¢ This Iuge answered of this , in his absence
I may nat ©eue , diffynytif sentence
Lat do hi~ calle , and I wol gladly heere
Thow shalt haue al rigút , and no wrongö here
¢ Virginius cam to wyte , the Iuges wille
And rigút a non was red , this cursed bille
The sentence of it , was as ©e shuln heere
¢ To ©ow©ow my lord Sire Apius , so dere
Shewetú ©oure pore êuaunt , deereClaudiusdeereClaudius
How that a knygút , called Virginius
Ageyns the lawe , a geyn al equytee
Holdetú expres , a geyn the wyl of me
My seruau~t , whicú that is my thral by rigút
Whicú fro myn hous , was stolen vp on a nygút
Whil that she was ful ©ongö , this wyl I preeue
By witnesse lord , so that it nat ©ow greue
She nys his dougúter nat , what so he seye
Wherfore to ©ow my lord , the Iuge , I preye
ñelde me my thral , if that it be ©oure wille
Lo this was al , the sentence , of his bille
Virginius gan , vp on the thral biholde
But hastily , er he his tale tolde
And wolde haue preued it , as shulde a knygút
And eke by wytnessynge , of many a wygút
That al was fals , that seide his aduersarie
This cursed Iuge , wolde no thyng tarie
Ne heere a word more , of virginius
But ©af his Iugement , and seide thus
I deme a noon this cherl , his seruau~t haue
Thow shalt no lengere , in thyn hous hir saue
